questions.question: Which ancient Indian text is considered the oldest among the Vedas?
questions.explanation: Rig Veda is the oldest of the four Vedas, composed around 1500–1200 BCE.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526 after the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Which movement was initiated by Mahatma Gandhi in 1942 calling for complete independence from British rule?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched in 1942 demanding an end to British rule.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Indian National Army (INA) during World War II?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose was the leader of the INA and a prominent freedom fighter.

questions.question: The Partition of India took place in which year?
questions.explanation: India was partitioned in 1947, leading to the creation of India and Pakistan.

questions.question: Which river is considered the longest in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari River is the longest river in India, approximately 1,465 km long.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is located mainly in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is primarily in Rajasthan.

questions.question: Which climate zone is predominant in the Western Ghats?
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats experience a tropical monsoon climate with heavy rainfall.

questions.question: Which mineral resource is found abundantly in Odisha?
questions.explanation: Odisha is rich in bauxite, used for aluminium production.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been the President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: The right to vote in India is a part of which fundamental right?
questions.explanation: The right to vote is included under the Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32).

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Panchayati Raj system?
questions.explanation: Articles 243 to 243-ZG deal with Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The independence of the judiciary is guaranteed by which fundamental right?
questions.explanation: Article 14 ensures equality before the law, safeguarding judicial independence.

questions.question: India's Union Budget is presented by the?
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister presents the Union Budget in Parliament.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the highest to India’s GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector is the largest contributor to India’s GDP.

questions.question: Which financial institution is responsible for monetary policy in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) formulates monetary policy.

questions.question: The 'Make in India' initiative aims to promote:
questions.explanation: 'Make in India' aims to boost manufacturing in India.

questions.question: Which law explains the phenomenon of electromagnetic induction?
questions.explanation: Faraday’s Law describes electromagnetic induction.

questions.question: Which element is most abundant in the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust.

questions.question: The process of photosynthesis occurs in which part of the plant?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis occurs mainly in the leaves of plants.

questions.question: Which space agency launched the Mars Rover named 'Perseverance'?
questions.explanation: NASA launched the Mars Rover 'Perseverance' in 2020.

questions.question: Which is the world's largest biodiversity hotspot?
questions.explanation: The Indo-Burma region is considered the world's largest biodiversity hotspot.

questions.question: The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) was adopted in which year?
questions.explanation: The CBD was adopted at the Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro in 1992.

questions.question: Which Indian law aims to protect the environment and forests?
questions.explanation: The Environment Protection Act, 1986, provides comprehensive regulations for environmental protection.

questions.question: Which endangered species was recently declared the 'state animal' of Odisha?
questions.explanation: The Olive Ridley Sea Turtle is recently declared Odisha's state animal.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi assumed office as CM of Odisha on 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: C. P. Radhakrishnan is the Vice President since 12 September 2025.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the External Affairs Minister since 2019.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been the President since 25 July 2022.
